title: Crunchy Smoky Canadian Snapper Fish Stir-fry
description: A tantalizing combination of smoky flavors and crunchy textures with a touch of sweetness to help cure hangovers, this dish is perfect for a quick and easy dinner.

ingredients.name: Snaps or fresh spring onions for garnish

instructions: Preheat your wok or skillet over medium-high heat. Heat olive oil until shimmering.
Add garlic cloves, onion, and chili flakes to the pan. Stir-fry for about 2 minutes until fragrant.
Add snapper fish fillets skin-side down. Season with white pepper and soy sauce. Cover partially with a lid and cook for about 3-4 minutes on each side or until cooked through.
Gently toss in bell peppers, kaffir lime leaves, and snap/spring onions. Continue to stir-fry for another 2 minutes ensuring all vegetables are tender but crisp.
Finish off by adding cilantro just before serving. Stir well to combine with the fish and vegetables. Serve hot.
To make it extra crunchy and smoky, you can sprinkle a bit of sesame seeds or smoked paprika on top.
